pages.text:
The First Rift

Legends of the Rift · Issue #1

When the Prime broke, a courier chose to keep.

Inside cover

Riftwilds Comic Publishing — original fantasy. No crypto required to read.

Credits & cosmetics may unlock along the way. The story itself stays free.

The First Rift

Issue #1

Elara Venn carries a damaged egg through the Fracture's first nights and names the path of keeping at the Riftstone.

Series says: Elara Venn · First Riftling · Bramblefox · Mossprig

Credits

Story · Riftwilds Lore Desk

Art · Commons Archive (official plates)

Lettering · Publishing Engine

Canon alignment: World Codex · Riftling Codex · Legends of the Rift TCG.

Previously in the Riftwilds…

Aeryndra's Prime Gateway failed. Layered roads learned to rewrite themselves. A courier carried a warm egg toward mud that would become the Commons.

Age of First Eggs → Founding of the Commons.

The sky tears

Aeryndra's Prime Gateway screamed once — then the world learned how to layer.

Nine days before the Commons had a name

Roads crossed deserts that had never met forests.

Elara Venn says: The egg is warm. That is enough to keep walking.

Elara Venn thinks: If I name myself hero, I will fail the small thing in my arms.

Stranger says: Take the crown road — safer.

Elara Venn says: Safer for whom?

She shared waybread with a child who had lost a name.

Elara Venn says: Call the creature yours to keep — not yours to own.

Codex echo: Waybread on the First Night

Gateway Hearts

Codex fragment — recovered from the margin.

Gateway Hearts: Hearts were living cores. Stones are travel shards. The Commons Riftstone is a fragment of the Prime — maps of places not yet found.

The Riftstone claims mud

Farmers, healers, and Keepers gathered where stable paths still intersected.

Mira (young) says: Is it… looking at me?

Elara Venn says: It is remembering the world. Help it stay gentle.

The Soft Exodus had closed into eggs. Battle culture would come later.

Village elder says: What do we call you?

Elara Venn says: Courier. Keeper, if the egg agrees.

Secret symbol: FIRST-LIGHT-01

First Riftling thinks: …keep…

Original IP note: Riftlings are living archives — not weapons-first. Preservation before prowess.

Forest Bond — living vine-light

Ability tease · Forest Bond

When Bramblefox chooses a Keeper, the grove answers with green thread — Forest Bond, not a leash.

Elara Venn says: It agreed. That is rarer than glowing.

Tease card: Bramblefox · Deck Atelier · Codex family-bramblefox

Years soft-shifted. Elara's bond slowed her aging — Spirit bleed, not magic vanity.

Elara Venn says: New Keepers still arrive with empty hands and full fear.

Elara Venn says: Good. Fear means they noticed the egg is becoming.

Play this chapter in Live World when you are ready.

Mud took bootprints. The Riftstone took names.

Healer says: We can raise walls. Or we can raise Keepers.

Elara Venn says: Raise Keepers who know which wall is a cradle.

Child says: Will the egg leave if I am scared?

Elara Venn says: Fear is allowed. Force is not.

That was the first Compact law — spoken before ink existed.

First Riftling thinks: …stay…

On the seventh night the Fracture roads rearranged again.

Scout says: East gate is west. West gate is… soup.

Elara Venn says: Then we walk by warmth, not by map.

First Riftling thinks: …warm…

Codex stub: Fracture roads

A stranger offered a crown-shard for the egg — bright, legal-looking, wrong.

Crown courier says: Name a price. The realm will keep it safer than mud.

Elara Venn says: The egg is not inventory. Leave.

Soft Exodus fragment

Codex fragment — recovered from the margin.

Soft Exodus fragment: When the Prime tore, living stories fled into shells. First Riftlings are archives that chose caretakers — not weapons that chose masters.

Village elder says: We will call this place Commons — shared ground.

Elara Venn says: Shared duty. Shared keeping.

The Riftstone accepted mud, names, and a courier who refused a throne.

First Riftling thinks: …home…

Something listens beyond the tear

As the Commons named itself, a second pulse answered from unmapped dark — not crown, not merchant. Cliffhanger: the Fracture was not finished speaking.

Elara Venn says: We keep what we can see. We prepare for what we cannot.

First Riftling thinks: …listen…

Issue #1 closes on a promise — and a pulse that will call Spark, Circus, and every Keeper after.

Next: Spark's Journey — Compact on the open road

Mud and names

Before walls, the Commons was a promise spoken over warm shells.

Elara Venn says: Carry the egg like a future, not a trophy.

Keeper thinks: If I rush the naming, I will miss the becoming.

Crown road refused

Safer roads asked for a price the egg could not pay.

Elara Venn says: Safer for whom?

Keeper thinks: A crown that buys consent is still theft.

Second pulse foreshadow

Beyond the first tear, something colder practiced waiting.

Elara Venn says: We keep what we can see.

Keeper thinks: The Fracture is not finished speaking.

Issue complete

You finished Issue #1: The First Rift.

Elara Venn carries a damaged egg through the Fracture's first nights and names the path of keeping at the Riftstone.

Play this chapter → Enter Live World — Commons

Shape the side-story

What should Elara's next courier route emphasize?

Care first says: More hatchery ethics scenes next arc.

Exploration says: Push toward unmapped Gateway paths.

Keeper & Companion Profiles

Elara Venn says: First Keeper — Courier who refused a crown and kept an egg.

First Riftling says: Companion — Living archive born from Soft Exodus fragment.

Bramblefox says: Grove companion — Forest Bond shown as living vine-light — game-accurate Nature skirmisher tease.

Open: /codex/riftlings/bramblefox

Mossprig says: Tracker — Grove-affinity pathfinder; soft rustle is dialogue, not noise.

Open: /codex/riftlings/mossprig
